ogTitle: Butlers Gorge vs Calder: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Butlers Gorge and Calder.
heading: Butlers Gorge vs Calder — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Butlers Gorge, Tasmania and Calder, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Butlers Gorge has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Calder (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Butlers Gorge or Calder safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Butlers Gorge has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Calder scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Butlers Gorge scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Calder scores 63/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Butlers Gorge or Calder?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Butlers Gorge has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
